name: Barbell Glute Bridge
resistance_profile: Floor-based hip extension with peak glute tension at the top.
execution_steps: Sit on the floor and roll a loaded, padded barbell over your hips.
Lie back with knees bent and feet flat, heels a few inches from your glutes.
Grip the bar to keep it steady, brace your core and tuck your ribs down.
Drive through your heels to lift the bar until your hips reach full extension.
Squeeze your glutes at the top without arching your lower back, then lower under control.
good_when: You want to load the glutes heavily but don't have a bench for hip thrusts.
You have outgrown the bodyweight bridge and need progressive overload.
You want a floor-based hip-extension option that keeps tension on the glutes.
bad_when: You have a bench available and want a fuller range of motion (choose the hip thrust).
You want a lengthened-biased glute stimulus (choose an RDL or Bulgarian split squat).
